mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of observations during three-month FEDFUNDS downtrends.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points triggers a rapid repricing of recession risk that reduces expected corporate earnings and drives negative 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day forward returns in more than 45% of instances following a UNRATE jump exceeding 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in real retail sales and housing starts within the same calendar month signal a synchronized collapse in consumer spending and residential investment that amplifies recession probabilities, leading to negative 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is contracting on a year-over-year basis, a month-over-month decline in the industrial production index confirms a deepening supply-side recession that compresses profit margins and generates negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: During INDPRO year-over-year contraction, month-over-month INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 forward returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales exceeding 0.5 percent signals resilient consumer demand that supports corporate revenue expectations and drives positive 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Following RSAFS growth exceeding 0.5% month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive less than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth exceeding 0.5 percent month-over-month during a period when the federal funds rate is above 4.0 percent signals an overheating economy that invites further Federal Reserve action, raising terminal rate fears and generating negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%, INDPRO increases exceeding 0.5% month-over-month are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 forward returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent foreshadows a collapse in construction activity and household wealth that spills over into broader consumer spending, leading to negative 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Following a month-over-month HOUST decline exceeding 5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 45% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls consistently exceeding 150 thousand indicate a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS increases exceed 150 thousand month-over-month, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive less than 55% of the time.
